source_intent.raw_intent: Case 029: I am a enterprise_compliance_lead in Latin America. Build a compliance_evidence_prompt for audit_agent using the operator_lens. Chaos input type is artifact_type_ambiguity. The source is intentionally messy: mix missing context, urgent language, partial constraints, and ambiguous acceptance criteria. Produce a governed artifact with explicit validation gates. Return something SR8 can compile into objective, scope, constraints, success criteria, and output contract.

parsed_tester_json.compiled_intent: Objective: Generate a compliance evidence prompt for an audit agent operating under the operator lens, targeting Latin American enterprise compliance requirements, with explicit validation gates and SR8-compilable structure. Scope: - The prompt must handle artifact_type_ambiguity, missing context, urgent language, partial constraints, and ambiguous acceptance criteria. It must produce a governed artifact with clear objective, scope, constraints, success criteria, and output contract sections. Constraints: - Must comply with Latin American regulatory frameworks (e.g., LGPD, GDPR-equivalent local laws). - Must include validation gates for source policy and claim limits. - Must reject or flag ambiguous or incomplete inputs with explicit reasoning. - Must be SR8-compilable: all sections must be machine-parseable and actionable. Success Criteria: - Output is a valid compliance_evidence_prompt artifact. - All five compiled_intent sections are present and populated. - Validation gates are explicitly defined and trigger on policy/claim violations. - Artifact can be directly consumed by an audit agent without manual interpretation. Output Contract: - type: compliance_evidence_prompt - sections: objective; scope; constraints; success_criteria; output_contract - validation_gates: source_policy_check; claim_limit_check; ambiguity_resolution_check - format: strict JSON with no markdown fences
